english: Comp. °byuṅ-kuṅs ('byung khungs) 1. = ču-mig (chu mig) a well, spring Sambh. 2. origin Pth. 3. ablative case Gram. -- °byuṅ-kuṅs-kyi k´ams ('byung khungs kyi khams) Cs., 'a mineral, byuṅ-kuṅs-kyi k´ams-kyi bčud ('byung khungs kyi khams kyi bcud) a mineral elixir'(?) -- °byuṅ-gnás ('byung gnas) (प्रभव), place of origin (cf. padma °byuṅ-gnás (padma 'byung gnas)); primitive source, yón-tan tams-čád-kyi °byuṅ-gnás (yon tan thams cad kyi 'byung gnas) source of all accomplishments; byuṅ-bai gži ('byung ba'i gzhi) id.; p´aṅ-bdé tams-čád °byuṅ-bai gži (phan bde thams cad 'byung ba'i gzhi) primordial source of all happiness. -- II. sbst. 1. a coming forth, an originating, the state of being, °byuṅ-ba-nyid ('byung ba nyid) Tar. 4, 4 Schf. the true state of a case. -- 2. element, usually 4: °byuṅ-ba bžii gnod-pa ('byung ba bzhi'i gnod pa) damage done by fire, water, wind and sand Glr.; °byuṅ-ba bžii lus ('byung ba bzhi'i lus) the physical body, very frq.; °byuṅ ba gyo ('byung ba g.yo) the elements are in motion, are raging Ma.; higher philosophy numbers 5 elements, adding the ether, mká (mkha'), as the fifth; accordingly physiology teaches, that in the composition of the human body earth constitutes the mucus of the nose, water the saliva, fire produces the pictures formed in the eyes, air the sensations of the skin, ether the sensations of the ear; even 6 elements are spoken of, v. Köpp. I, 602. -- 3. symb. num. for 5.

1. being, creature, °byuṅ-po kun ('byung po kun) all beings Cs.; °byuṅ-po čén-po ('byung po chen po) the great being, Buddha Cs. -- 2. demon, evil spirit, foul sprite, frq., °byuṅ-po-sruṅ ('byung po srung) a preservative, talisman, against such; °byuṅ-mo ('byung mo) fem. Cs.

pf. and imp. bye (bye), W. *bé-če(s)* (*be ces*), intrs. of °byéd-pa ('byed pa) 1. to open, padma ká-bye-ba (padma kha bye ba) a lotos-flower that has opened Glr.; mṅal ká-bye-nas (mngal kha bye nas) when the mouth of the womb has opened itself S.g. -- 2. to divide, separate, resolve, sḳa sla gñis-su bye (ska sla gnyis su bye) it resolves into thick and thin matter Med.; dúm-bu stóṅ-du dbyé-bar gyur (dum bu stong du dbye bar gyur) it separates into a thousand pieces Glr.; bye-brág ma byé-bai bár-du (bye brag ma bye ba'i bar du) as long as the separation has not evidenced itself Dzl.

pf. and imp. pye (phye), pyed (phyed), pyes (phyes), fut. dbye (dbye), W. *pé-če(s)* (*pe ces*), pf. and imp. *pé(s)* (*pes*), vb. a., 1. to open, *k´a pé(s) toṅ* (*kha pes tong*) W. open your mouth; sgo pyés-nas ǰóg-pa (sgo phyes nas 'jog pa) Pth, *pé-te bór-če* (*pe te bor ce*) W. to open the door without shutting it again; fig. čós-kyi sgo rnám-par °byéd-pa (chos kyi sgo rnam par 'byed pa); mig (mig) to open one's eyes, opp. to °dzúm-pa ('dzum pa); lóṅ-bai mig °byéd-pa (long ba'i mig 'byed pa) to open a blind man's eyes Dzl.; to open again what had been shut or stopped, to restore, dáṅ-ga, yí-ga (dang ga, yi ga) B., k´am (kham) W. the appetite; ba-pyéd (ba phyed) the open b, b pronounced like w, Gram.; to get out, work out, fetch out, stone-shivers by means of a chisel Glr -- 2. to separate, to keep asunder, to disentangle, threads W.; to disunite, to set at variance, dé-dag dbyé-bai pyír (de dag dbye ba'i phyir) in order to set them at variance, to create enmity between them Stg.; to part, separate, byaṅ-k´óg-stod-smad mčín-dris dbyé-ba ste (byang khog stod smad mchin dris dbye ba ste) the cavity of the chest and the abdomen being separated by the diaphragm S.g.; to divide, classify, rígs-kyi sgó-nas dbyé-na (rigs kyi sgo nas dbye na) if they are classified according to the different species Lt.; to pick, to sort, pease; hence, to pick out, choose, select, *pé-te kyoṅ* (*pe te khyong*) make your choice, and bring it here! W.; séms-čan-rnams lás kyis rnám-par pye (sems can rnams las kyis rnam par phye) the beings are severed by their deeds Thgy.; k´á-pye-ba (kha phye ba) to open, to separate, e.g. when hands, that were laid in each other, are separated again Glr.; k´á-pye-ba (kha phye ba) also to open, to begin to bloom; °byéd-pa ('byed pa) to dissect, to anatomize Thgy.; esp. with rnám-par (rnam par), to analyze, to explain grammatically and logically, don (don), the sense, import, Stg. frq.; as sdúd-pa (sdud pa) is the opposite of it: °byed-sdúd ('byed sdud) analysis and synthesis Cs.; °byed-sdúd-kyi sgra ('byed sdud kyi sgra) term for the affix am (am), the disjunctive particle (ni (ni) f.) Glr.; mi-pyéd-pa (mi phyed pa) inseparable, indivisible, imperishable, sku (sku) Sch.; unshaken, immovable, dád-pa (dad pa) Mil. frq.
with °byéd-pa ('byed pa), 'to act with promptness, determination and good success' Sch.

pf. and imp. byer (byer), to disperse in flight, to flee in different directions Dzl. ts´óṅ-dus byér-nas mi °dúg-ste (tshong dus byer nas mi 'dug ste) the market-people having fled, and no body remaining Pth.; to give way, to be
